_comment: WC2026 Group I, MD3. Standings before: France 6 (3-1 Senegal, 3-1 Iraq), Norway 6 (4-1 Iraq, 3-1 Senegal), Iraq 0, Senegal 0. BOTH already qualified -> this decides 1st vs 2nd (seeding only). Two attacking sides (Norway scored 7, France 6 in two games) -> likely an open, higher-scoring game. MOTIVATION CAVEAT (post-Mexico/South-Africa lesson): both are through, but do NOT apply a rotation discount -- trust the strength baseline; a small nudge at most. France the marginally better side; Norway (Haaland) dangerous. Near-even, France a hair ahead. Home = Norway, away = France. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Both already qualified on 6 points; this decides who tops the group (seeding). France marginally the better side; Norway (Haaland) a live threat. Open, attacking game likely.

home.name: Norway
notes: Haaland-led, scored 7 in two games (vs weaker Iraq/Senegal). attack 1.22.
Decent but not elite at the back vs France. defense 0.92.
Already through; open game, no motivation downgrade applied.

away.name: France
notes: Elite; won both 3-1. attack 1.35, marginally the better side.
Through as well; trust the strength baseline, not a rotation guess.

_postmatch.result: France 4-1 Norway. France top the group; an open, high-scoring game as called.
_postmatch.model_1x2: Norway 34.2 / Draw 25.1 / France 40.7 (LOW, coin-flip with goals)
_postmatch.verdict: 1X2 hit (the slight favourite France won) AND the game-shape read landed perfectly: I called this the open, high-scoring match (Over 2.5 58.8%, Over 3.5 36.4%, BTTS 61.6%) and it was 4-1 -- 5 goals, both scoring. France overperformed (1.60 -> 4) so the 4-1 margin was the finishing tail, but on a LOW-confidence coin-flip the value was the open/goals read, which was spot on. No rotation discount was applied despite both being qualified, and that was correct -- France went full.
_postmatch.lesson: Validates the no-motivation-discount rule for qualified teams (post-Mexico): they can still go full. And the open/Over/BTTS read on two attacking sides was right -- texture beats side on a coin-flip.
